content.text:
Every sound you hear — music, voices, thunder, a dog barking — is made by VIBRATIONS!

🎵 What is a vibration?
A vibration is a very fast back-and-forth motion. When something vibrates, it makes the air around it move too. Those air movements travel to your ears as SOUND WAVES.

Try this: put your hand on your throat and hum. You can feel the vibrations!

🔊 Loud and Soft Sounds:
A big vibration makes a LOUD sound.
A small vibration makes a SOFT sound.
We measure loudness in DECIBELS (dB).
• 0–30 dB: very quiet (whisper, rustling leaves)
• 60 dB: normal talking
• 100+ dB: very loud (concert, jet plane) — can hurt your ears!

🎶 High and Low Sounds (Pitch):
Pitch is how high or low a sound is.
• A short, tight guitar string makes a HIGH pitch
• A long, loose guitar string makes a LOW pitch
• Birds chirp at a high pitch; a tuba plays a low pitch

🌊 How does sound travel?
Sound travels through AIR — and also through water and solids! Sound cannot travel through empty space (that's why space is silent). Sound travels about 343 meters per second in air — much slower than light!

Ears are specially shaped to collect sound waves and send them to the brain.

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing sound waves traveling from a vibrating object to an ear

experiment.instructions: Pluck the rubber band and watch the vibrations create sound waves!

combinations.resultLabel: The rubber band vibrates and makes sound! The empty box amplifies it — the air inside the box resonates with the vibrations, making the sound louder. This is how guitars work!

practiceQuestions.hint: Put your hand on your throat when you hum — what do you feel?
